title: acer cappadocicum subsp. savatieri
intro: Acer cappadocicum subsp. savatieri, also known as the Cappadocian maple or the Himalayan maple, is a subspecies of the Acer cappadocicum maple tree native to the Himalayas and mountainous regions of Central Asia. The bark of this plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as tannins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Acer cappadocicum subsp. savatieri have been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its bark, which can be dried and infused in hot water or used in the form of extracts and tinctures.
